title: Ensure that the OpenShift PKI certificate file permissions are set to 600 or more restrictive

input_row.title: Ensure that the OpenShift PKI certificate file permissions are set to 600 or more restrictive
input_row.description: Ensure that OpenShift PKI certificate files have permissions of 644 or more restrictive.
input_row.rationale: OpenShift makes use of a number of certificate files as part of the operation of its components. The permissions on these files should be set to 644 or more restrictive to protect their integrity.
input_row.audit: Certificates for control plane components like kube-apiserver , kube-controller- manager , and kube-scheduler are stored in the directory /etc/kubernetes/static- pod-certs/secrets . Certificate files all have permissions 600 . Run the following command. # Should 600 or more restrictive for i in $(oc -n openshift-kube-apiserver get pod -l app=openshift-kube- apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}') do echo $i static-pod-certs oc exec -n openshift-kube-apiserver $i -c kube-apiserver -- \ find /etc/kubernetes/static-pod-certs -type f -wholename '*/secrets/*.crt' -exec stat -c %a {} \; done Verify that the permissions are 600 .
input_row.remediation: No remediation required; file permissions are managed by the operator.
input_row.default_value: By default, the certificates used by OpenShift are set to have permissions of 600 . Internal Only - General

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Check the OpenShift certificate file permissions:**
   - Use the IBM Cloud CLI to connect to your OpenShift cluster. You can execute the command in your terminal: 
     ```
     oc -n openshift-kube-apiserver get pod -l app=openshift-kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}'
     ```
   - This command retrieves the pod names associated with the OpenShift kube-apiserver.

2. **Execute the command within the pod:**
   - Once you have the pod names, run the command:
     ```
     oc exec -n openshift-kube-apiserver <pod_name> -c kube-apiserver -- find /etc/kubernetes/static-pod-certs -type f -wholename '*/secrets/*.crt' -exec stat -c %a {} \;
     ```
   - Replace `<pod_name>` with the actual name of the pod obtained from the previous command.

3. **Verify the permissions output:**
   - Review the output of the command to check the permissions of the certificate files. The expected output should display permission value as `600` or more restrictive (e.g., `400` or `500`).

4. **Confirm compliance:**
   - If all displayed certificate files have permissions of `600` or less, they are compliant with the control. If any have permissions greater than `600`, they are non-compliant.

By following these steps, you will verify the compliance of OpenShift PKI certificate file permissions as required by the control.
